summary: Backend-only verification of the members-sync bug fix in POST/PATCH /api/users. All 4 requested scenarios passed: user creation auto-adds a member record, PATCH updates the member name, existing endpoints still return 200, and manager (no group_id) does NOT create a member record.
passed: POST /api/users (role=member, group_id=<gid>) -> 200; NEW member record present in GET /api/groups/{gid}/members with matching name 'عضو اختبار' and user_id === created user's id
PATCH /api/users/{id} {name:'الاسم الجديد'} -> 200; GET /api/groups/{gid}/members reflects updated name for the same member record id
Sanity: POST /api/users (role=manager, no group_id) -> 200 and NO member record created in any of the 4 groups
notes: Login response field is 'token' (not 'access_token'). All calls used Authorization: Bearer <token> against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL/api. Group id used for primary test: f002d2c8-cd3f-4271-9f67-b50dc6870c57; new member user id: 1ea6fb94-871b-47f4-8d60-f6db70714485; member record id: f37b7ea3-7b35-48f6-9318-2d52c4c083e1.
